passages.title: Fishbourne Roman Palace
passages.source_html:
Fishbourne Roman Palace


A


Fishbourne Roman Palace is in the village of Fishbourne in West Sussex, England. This large palace was built in the 1st century AD, around thirty years after the Roman conquest of Britain, on the site of Roman army grain stores which had been established after the invasion, in the reign of the Roman Emperor Claudius in 43 AD. The rectangular palace was built around formal gardens, the northern half of which have been reconstructed. There were extensive alterations in the 2nd and 3rd centuries AD, with many of the original black and white mosaic floors being overlaid with more sophisticated coloured ones, including a perfectly preserved mosaic of a dolphin in the north wing. More alterations were in progress when the palace burnt down in around 270 AD, after which it was abandoned.


B


Local people had long believed that a Roman palace once existed in the area. However, it was not until 1960 that the archaeologist Barry Cunliffe, of Oxford University, first systematically excavated the site, after workmen had accidentally uncovered a wall while they were laying a water main. The Roman villa excavated by Cunliffe's team was so grand that it became known as Fishbourne Roman Palace, and a museum was erected to preserve some of the remains. This is administered by the Sussex Archaeological Society.


C


In its day, the completed palace would have comprised four large wings with colonnaded fronts. The north and east wings consisted of suites of private rooms built around courtyards, with a monumental entrance in the middle of the east wing. In the north-east corner there was an assembly hall. The west wing contained state rooms, a large ceremonial reception room, and a gallery. The south wing contained the owner's private apartments. The palace included as many as 50 mosaic floors, under-floor central heating and a bathhouse. In size, Fishbourne Palace would have been approximately equivalent to some of the great Roman palaces of Italy, and was by far the largest known Roman residence north of the European Alps, at about 500 feet (150m) square. A team of volunteers and professional archaeologists are involved in an ongoing archaeological excavation on the site of nearby, possibly military, buildings.


D


The first buildings to be erected on the site were constructed in the early part of the conquest in 43 AD. Later, two timber buildings were constructed, one with clay and mortar floors and plaster walls, which appears to have been a house of some comfort. These buildings were demolished in the 60s AD and replaced by a substantial stone house, which included colonnades, and a bath suite. It has been suggested that the palace itself, incorporating the previous house in its south-east corner, was constructed around 73-75 AD. However, Dr Miles Russell, of Bournemouth University, reinterpreted the ground plan and the collection of objects found and has suggested that, given the extremely close parallels with the imperial palace of Domitian in Rome, its construction may more plausibly date to after 92 AD.


E


With regard to who lived in Fishbourne Palace, there are a number of theories; for example, one proposed by Professor Cunliffe is that, in its early phase, the palace was the residence of Tiberius Claudius Cogidubnus, a local chieftain who supported the Romans, and who may have been installed as king of a number of territories following the first stage of the conquest. Cogidubnus is known from a reference to his loyalty in Agricola, a work by the Roman writer Tacitus, and from an inscription commemorating a temple dedicated to the gods Neptune and Minerva found in the nearby city of Chichester. Another theory is that it was built for Sallustius Lucullus, a Roman governor of Britain of the late 1st century, who may have been the son of the British prince Adminius. Two inscriptions recording the presence of Lucullus have been found in Chichester, and the redating by Miles Russell of the palace to the early 90s AD would fit far more securely with such an interpretation. If the palace was designed for Lucullus, then it may have only been in use for a few years, as the Roman historian Suetonius records that Lucullus was executed by the Emperor Domitian in or shortly after 93 AD.


F


Additional theories suggest that either Verica, a British king of the Roman Empire in the years preceding the Claudian invasion, was owner of the palace, or Tiberius Claudius Catuarus, following the recent discovery of a gold ring belonging to him. The palace outlasted the original owner, whoever he was, and was extensively re-planned early in the 2nd century AD, and subdivided into a series of lesser apartments. Further redevelopment was begun in the late 3rd century AD, but these alterations were incomplete when the north wing was destroyed in a fire in around 270 AD. The damage was too great to repair, and the palace was abandoned and later dismantled.


G


A modern museum had been built by the Sussex Archaeological Society, incorporating most of the visible remains, including one wing of the palace. The gardens have been re-planted using authentic plants from the Roman period.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 6


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1

Fishbourne Palace was the first structure to be built on its site.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位关键词：

first structure


对应原文：

A段，“This large palace was built in the 1st century AD, around thirty years after the Roman conquest of Britain, on the site of Roman army grain stores which been established after the invasion, in the reign of the Roman Emperor Claudius in 43 AD.”


解析：

原文提到palace是罗马人征服英国后30年左右建造的，而在罗马人入侵英国后先在当地建立了grain stores。


同义替换：

structure=built/established

2

Fishbourne Palace was renovated more than once.

正确答案： A. TRUE

定位关键词：

renovated more than once


原文：

A段，“The rectangular palace was built around formal gardens, the northern half of which have been reconstructed. There were extensive alterations in the 2nd and 3rd centuries AD, with many of the original black and white mosaic floors being overlaid with more sophisticated coloured ones, including a perfectly preserved mosaic of a dolphin in the north wing. More alterations were in progress when the palace burnt down in around 270 AD, after which it was abandoned.”


解析：

原文提到宫殿北半部有过重建（reconstruct）；公元2、3世纪有过大规模翻新（alteration）；公元270年左右，这座宫殿被烧毁，后来又进行了更多的改建，表示翻新（renovate）不止一次。


同义替换：

renovate=reconstruct，alteration

3

Fishbourne Palace was large in comparison with Roman palaces in Italy.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位关键词：

Roman palace，Italy


原文：

C段，“In size, Fishbourne Palace would have been approximately equivalent to some of the great Roman palaces of Italy”


解析：

原文提到在规模上，Fishbourne Palace大约相当于意大利一些伟大的罗马宫殿，并没有指出Fishbourne Palace 更大。

4

Research is continuing in the area close to Fishbourne Palace.

正确答案： A. TRUE

定位关键词：

Research，area close to Fishbourne Palace


原文：

C段，“A team of volunteers and professional archaeologists are involved in an ongoing archaeological excavation on the site of nearby, possibly military, buildings.”


解析：

原文提到一个由志愿者和专业考古学家组成的团队正在对附近可能是军事建筑的遗址进行考古发掘。


同义替换：

nearby=close to；ongoing=continuing

5

Researchers agree on the identity of the person for whom Fishbourne Palace was constructed.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位关键词：

identity of the person, constructed for


原文：

E段，“With regard to who lived in Fishbourne Palace, there are a number of theories...”


解析：

原文提到Professor Cunliffe认为Cogidubnus曾经住在Fishbourne Palace，而有些科学家认为Fishbourne Palace是为Sallustius Lucullus设计。因此，研究者在Fishbourne Palace到底是为谁建造这件事上并没有达成共识。


同义替换：

construct=built

6

Fishbourne Palace was burnt down by local people.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

定位关键词：

burnt down


原文：

F段，“Further redevelopment was begun in the late 3rd century AD, but these alterations were incomplete when the north wing wadestroyed in a fire in around 270 AD.”


解析：

原文只提到palace在大火中被摧毁，并未提到是被local people放火摧毁。

Questions 7 - 13


Complete the notes. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

7

Fishbourne Palace

Construction

   •    The first buildings on the site contained food for the

正确答案： 7 Roman army##army
Question 7

定位关键词：first building，food


原文：A段，“This large palace was built in the 1st century AD, around thirty years after the Roman conquest of Britain, on the site of Roman army grain stores which had been established after the invasion, in the reign of the Roman Emperor Claudius in 43 AD.”


解析：原文提到在罗马人入侵英国后先在当地建立了军用粮仓Roman army grain stores。


同义替换：food=grain

8

   •    The palace building surrounded  

正确答案： 8 gardens##formal gardens
Question 8

定位关键词：surrounded


原文：A段，“The rectangular palace was built around formal gardens, the northern half of which have been reconstructed.”


解析：原文提到长方形宫殿围绕花园建造。


同义替换：surround=around

9

  •    In the 2nd and 3rd centuries, colour was added to the   of the palace.

正确答案： 9 floors##mosaic floors
Question 9

定位关键词：2nd，3rd，colour


原文：A段，“There were extensive alterations in the 2nd and 3rd centuries AD, with many of the original black and white mosaic floors being overlaid with more sophisticated coloured ones, including a perfectly preserved mosaic of a dolphin in the north wing.”


解析：原文提到黑白马赛克地板被更复杂的彩色马赛克地板覆盖。


同义替换：coloured ones指代mosaic floor。

10

Discovery

   •    The first part of the palace to be found was a  

正确答案： 10 wall
Question 10

定位关键词：first part


原文：B段，“However, it was not until 1960 that the archaeologist Barry Cunliffe, of Oxford University, first systematically excavated the site, after workmen had accidentally uncovered a wall while they were laying a water main.”


解析：原文提到在工人们铺设水管时意外发现一堵墙后，这个遗址才被第一次系统挖掘，所以宫殿被发现的第一个部分是wall。


同义替换：uncover=to be found

11

Possible inhabitants

   •    Cogidubnus – he is named in several writings

   •    Sallustius Lucullus – he may have lived there until approximately   AD

正确答案： 11 93
Question 11

定位关键词：Sallustius Lucullus，AD


原文：E段，“ If the palace was designed for Lucullus, then it may have only been in use for a few years, as the Roman historian Suetonius records that Lucullus was executed by the Emperor Domitian in or shortly after 93 AD.”


解析：原文指出Sallustius Lucullus在93 AD被处决。

12

   •    Verica – a British king

   •    Catuarus – his   was found there

正确答案： 12 gold ring##ring
Question 12

定位关键词：Catuarus


原文：F段，“Additional theories suggest that either Verica, a British king of the Roman Empire in the years preceding the Claudian invasion, was owner of the palace, or Tiberius Claudius Catuarus, following the recent discovery of a gold ring belonging to him.”


解析：原文提到发现了一枚属于Catuarus的gold ring。


同义替换：find=discovery

13

Present Day

   •    A   has been built on the site to help protect it.

正确答案： 13 modern museum##museum
Question 13

定位关键词：present day


原文：G段，“A modern museum had been built by the Sussex Archaeological Society, incorporating most of the visible remains, including one wing of the palace.”


解析：原文提到博物馆保存了大部分可见的遗迹。
